texto: Your soul may belong to Jesus, but your ass belongs to the marines.
explicacao: Citação de Eugene B. Sledge sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Your soul may belong to Jesus, but your ass belongs to the marines.” articula reflexao, jesus, marines em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como marines.

texto: As the sun disappeared below the horizon and its glare no longer reflected off a glassy sea, I thought of how beautiful the sunsets always were in the Pacific. They were even more beautiful than over Mobile Bay. Suddenly a thought hit me like a thunderbolt. Would I live to see the sunset tomorrow?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Eugene B. Sledge: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “As the sun disappeared below the horizon and its glare no longer reflected off a glassy sea, I th…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, sea, the-pacific pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de the-pacific e war.

texto: Something in me died at Peleliu. Perhaps it was the childish innocence that accepted as faith the claim that Man is basically good. Possibly I lost faith that politicians in high places, who do not have to endure war's savagery, will ever stop blundering and sending others to endure it.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Eugene B. Sledge sobre temas ligados a familia. A afirmação “Something in me died at Peleliu. Perhaps it was the childish innocence that accepted as faith the…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, battle, dissillusionment, útil em momentos de termino e luto.

Possibly I lost faith that politicians in high places, who do not have to endure war's savagery, will ever stop blundering and sending others to endure it.

texto: As I crawled out of the abyss of combat and over the rail of the Sea Runner, I realized that compassion for the sufferings of others is a burden to those who have it. As Wilfred Owen's poem "Insensibility" puts it so well, those who feel most of others suffer most in war.
explicacao: Citação de Eugene B. Sledge sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “As I crawled out of the abyss of combat and over the rail of the Sea Runner, I realized that comp…” articula reflexao, compassion, suffering em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como suffering e war.

frase_original: As I crawled out of the abyss of combat and over the rail of the Sea Runner, I realized that compassion for the sufferings of others is a burden to those who have it. As Wilfred Owen's poem "Insensibility" puts it so well, those who feel most of others suffer most in war.

texto: To the non-combatants and those on the periphery of action, the war meant only boredom or occasional excitement, but to those who entered the meat grinder itself the war was a netherworld of horror from which escape seemed less and less likely as casualties mounted and the fighting dragged on and on. Time had no meaning, life had no meaning. The fierce struggle for survival in the abyss of Peleliu had eroded the veneer of civilization and made savages of us all.
explicacao: Citação de Eugene B. Sledge sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “To the non-combatants and those on the periphery of action, the war meant only boredom or occasio…” articula reflexao, boredom, horror em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como horror e time.
